name: pages
context: Create, read, update, and archive Notion pages via the API
info: This skill covers the full lifecycle of Notion pages: creating new pages under a parent page or database, reading page properties and content, updating titles and property values, and archiving pages. Pages in Notion are composed of properties (title, rich text, dates, selects, relations, etc.) and content blocks (paragraphs, headings, lists, etc.). The API supports setting all property types at creation or update time, and appending child blocks for page content. Use @notionhq/client to interact with the Pages endpoints.
triggers: add content to a notion page

sections.title: Create pages
sections.body: POST to `/v1/pages` with `parent` (page_id or database_id), `properties` (must include title), and optional `children` (array of block objects). When creating under a database, properties must match the database schema. The response includes the new `page_id` and `url`. Use `notion.pages.create()` in the SDK. See [[notion/databases]] for database-backed page creation.

sections.title: Read and update pages
sections.body: Retrieve a page with `GET /v1/pages/{page_id}` or `notion.pages.retrieve()`. The response contains all properties but not content blocks — use `notion.blocks.children.list()` for content (see [[notion/blocks#append-children]]). Update properties with `PATCH /v1/pages/{page_id}` or `notion.pages.update()`. Only include the properties you want to change. The `last_edited_time` updates automatically.

sections.title: Property types
sections.body: Properties follow the parent database schema. Title is always required. Rich text, number, select, multi-select, date, checkbox, URL, email, phone, relation, rollup, formula, people, files, and created/last-edited time are all supported. Each type has a specific JSON format. Select uses `{ name: 'value' }`, dates use ISO 8601 strings, relations use arrays of page IDs. See [[notion/databases#property-types]] for the database schema side.

sections.title: Content blocks
sections.body: Page content is an ordered list of blocks. Append blocks at creation time via the `children` parameter or after creation via `notion.blocks.children.append()`. Blocks can be nested (e.g., toggle contents, list sub-items). See [[notion/blocks#block-types]] for supported types and [[notion/blocks#nesting]] for nesting rules.
